_notes: VERIFIED: ran `node dist/bin/edgewell.js bench` on 2026-06-18 and observed the human text shown in `cli_human_output`.
The bench command iterates 3 trials through `llm.stream(...)` against the linked QVAC SDK stub which yields a 12-token placeholder reply per trial.
Because the stub returns the token stream instantly, the wall-clock per-trial is sub-millisecond. The reported throughput is therefore an artifact of the stub, not a real model benchmark.

The `additional_benchmarks` block below measures four operations that DO use real local code (`rag.search`, `route`, `encryptRoundTrip`, `vector.search`) and reports median + p95 across 3 trials each.
On a real device loading llama.cpp with the LLAMA_3_2_1B_INST_Q4_0 model, expect per-trial wall time in the 200-2000 ms range (depending on prompt size and quantization) — see docs/PERFORMANCE.md.
To re-run: `node dist/bin/edgewell.js bench` — the header and three trial lines below are reproduced exactly.
prompt: Briefly describe what a healthy morning routine looks like.

primary.name: edgewell-bench
primary.stub_warning: samples are sub-millisecond because the linked QVAC SDK stub returns tokens instantly. Throughput NOT meaningful — see additional_benchmarks for real local operations.

additional_benchmarks._note: Median + p95 across 3 trials of operations that exercise real local code (RAG, router, crypto, vector). All numbers measured on the submission host (Apple M4 Max, 128 GiB, macOS 26.5).
